notes: Patches from Cycle 2 reviews WIN-011.c2 and WIN-012.c2. ISS-499/500 combined into a single WIN-012 detail_evidence clarification. ISS-501 removes stray GRACE sentence from WIN-011 detail_extra.

patches.find: The derivation is also circular: assume the Mohe anomaly is real → calculate κ → claim κ as a confirmed parameter.
patches.replace: The derivation is also circular: assume the Mohe anomaly is real → calculate κ → claim κ as a confirmed parameter. A further structural problem undermines the framing of 'two independent eclipse events confirming the same coupling constant': the numerator (−10.9 nT magnetic anomaly) and denominator (−6.5 μGal gravity anomaly) of κ come from entirely different eclipses at different locations — BOU (Boulder, Colorado, 2017) for the magnetic measurement and Mohe (northeast China, 1997) for the gravity measurement. BOU 2017 produced no simultaneous gravity measurement; Mohe 1997 produced no simultaneous magnetic field record. No single eclipse event measured both fields at the same site. A genuine coupling constant requires simultaneous measurement of both coupled quantities at the same location during the same event — otherwise the ratio is a post-hoc assembly of disparate datasets, not a physical relationship. The 'stability' of the ratio across two eclipses is therefore vacuous: if only the Mohe gravity measurement is contested (as WIN-013/014 suggest), κ has no independently validated denominator at all.
patches.rationale: ISS-499 (major): The dome claims 'two independent eclipse events confirming the same coupling constant' but the numerator and denominator of kappa come from different eclipses at different locations with different instruments. ISS-500 (major): Addresses dome defender's ratio-stability argument by explaining why the ratio cannot be considered stable when no single eclipse measured both fields simultaneously.

patches.find: The GRACE L1A claim conflates atmospheric drag with gravitational signal.
patches.rationale: ISS-501 (moderate): This GRACE sentence is carryover from the parked Section 3.6 (Dielectric Infographic, V4.9.3). WIN-011 is about ground-based gravimeter measurements during solar eclipses; GRACE is a satellite gravity mapping mission with no connection to eclipse gravity anomalies. The sentence is misplaced and confusing.
